content:
{
  "title": "Self-Verification Chain-of-Thought Prompting for Robust Mathematical Reasoning",
  "problem": "Large language models often generate mathematically incorrect solutions due to local reasoning errors or hallucination, even when using chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting. Existing CoT prompts do not reliably catch or correct these mistakes during generation.",
  "motivation": "Mathematical problem solving remains a frontier for LLM capabilities, with applications in education and STEM automation. As models are increasingly deployed in high-stakes, math-intensive domains, improving their accuracy and robustness is urgent. Enhanced prompt methods can offer a scalable way to boost mathematical reasoning without retraining.",
  "method": "We propose a 'Self-Verification Chain-of-Thought' (SV-CoT) prompting method. After producing a step-by-step CoT solution, the model is prompted to explicitly re-examine each intermediate step, stating whether it is valid and, if not, suggesting corrections. The final answer is only produced after all steps are marked verified. This iterative prompt structure encourages reflection and error detection, leveraging the LLM’s latent verification abilities.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate SV-CoT on GSM8K, MATH, and MiniF2F datasets. Baselines include standard CoT and least-to-most prompting. Metrics are final answer accuracy and intermediate step consistency. Human annotation will verify the quality of verification steps and correction rates."
}
